items.name: Mission Briefing & Environment Setup
items.description: Get the tenant agent-ready: Azure subscriptions, Fabric trial capacity, tooling, and the whole team onboarded.

items.name: Light Up the Agents
items.description: Deploy the Foundry agent workload on Container Apps + Cosmos DB and confirm telemetry is flowing to App Insights.

items.name: Build the Telemetry Landing Zone
items.description: Land cost, metrics, logs, metadata & diagnostics in ADLS Gen2 as one analytics-ready landing zone.

items.name: Connect Fabric to the Enterprise
items.description: Bring enterprise data into Fabric with zero copy — a Lakehouse, OneLake shortcuts, and Cosmos DB Mirroring.

items.name: Refine the Signal
items.description: Engineer a Bronze → Silver → Gold medallion pipeline that correlates reliability, cost, and performance signals.

items.name: Stand Up the Control Tower
items.description: Build a Direct Lake semantic model and the Reliability / Cost / Performance dashboards — the single pane of glass.

items.name: Make It Operational
items.description: Stretch goal: real-time alerts with Data Activator, FinOps chargeback, row-level security, and automation.

items.name: Agent Workload
items.description: Full-stack Foundry agent app — frontend, backend API, and agent orchestration you light up in Challenge 1.

items.name: Observability Ingestion
items.description: The landing zone that collects telemetry, cost, and metadata into an ADLS Gen2 data lake.

items.name: Fabric Control Tower
items.description: Workspace setup, medallion notebooks, pipelines, and the Direct Lake semantic model that powers the dashboards.

items.name: Observability SDK
items.description: Shared Python library that enforces a consistent OpenTelemetry event schema across every agent.

items.name: Reference Architecture
items.description: The end-to-end architecture and the four data stages — Emit, Ingest, Process, Serve — you build challenge by challenge.

items.name: Prerequisites
items.description: Azure roles, Fabric trial capacity, and local tooling to set up with your coach before Day 1.

items.name: FAQ
items.description: Frequently asked questions on cost, capacity, model choice, and running the event.

items.name: Coach Handbook
items.description: Logistics, timing, sample agenda, facilitation philosophy, and the reference-reveal policy for instructors.

items.name: Per-Challenge Coach Guides
items.description: Reference solutions, key commands, common pitfalls, and talking points for all seven challenges.
